{
  "name": "Hypergraph removal lemma",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": null,
    "doi": "10.1073/pnas.0502771102",
    "citation": "V. Rodl, B. Nagle, J. Skokan, M. Schacht, and Y. Kohayakawa, The hypergraph regularity method and its applications, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U.S.A. 102(23):8109-8113, 2005.",
    "year": 2005
  },
  "home_domain": "extremal_combinatorics",
  "what_it_does": "The hypergraph removal lemma says that a large finite uniform hypergraph with few copies of a fixed small hypergraph can be made copy-free by deleting few edges. It is a general-purpose counting/deletion principle behind several density results, including multidimensional Szemeredi-type theorems and corner configurations.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "a fixed k-uniform hypergraph H whose copies encode the target structured configuration",
    "a finite ambient hypergraph sequence with vertex sets growing to size n",
    "an asymptotic upper bound saying the ambient hypergraphs contain few copies of H",
    "a deletion/removal interpretation connecting removal of hyperedges to elimination of the target number-theoretic configurations",
    "a density or pseudorandomness framework strong enough to model the relevant set inside a grid or product space"
  ],
  "output_signature": "For fixed H and epsilon, constants delta and n0 such that any n-vertex k-uniform hypergraph with at most delta*n^v(H) copies of H can be made H-free by deleting at most epsilon*n^k edges.",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "a fixed k-uniform hypergraph H whose copies encode the target structured configuration",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Set.IsAPOfLength identifies the 3-term AP configuration, but no hypergraph encoding is present."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a finite ambient hypergraph sequence with vertex sets growing to size n",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "an asymptotic upper bound saying the ambient hypergraphs contain few copies of H",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a deletion/removal interpretation connecting removal of hyperedges to elimination of the target number-theoretic configurations",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a density or pseudorandomness framework strong enough to model the relevant set inside a grid or product space",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "Powerful numbers form a sparse one-dimensional sequence, while the technique is naturally dense/asymptotic in finite hypergraphs or grids.",
    "The target asks for finiteness of APs among consecutive powerful numbers, not for existence of configurations in a positive-density set.",
    "Consecutiveness in the ordered sequence of powerful numbers has no evident hypergraph-removal deletion analogue.",
    "The supplied statement contains no multidimensional grid model or pseudorandom majorant for powerful numbers."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.1,
  "source_analog_id": "A2"
}
